description:
Ensure existing registry records (which lack `executionMode`) are normalized to `worktree` mode on load, preserving backward compatibility.

## What to implement

In `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/registry.ts`:

When loading a `WorkerRuntime` record that has no `executionMode` field:
1. Set `executionMode = "worktree"`
2. Set `checkoutPath = worktreePath` (it already has `worktreePath`)
3. Preserve all existing fields as-is
4. Write the normalized version back if the registry is saved

## Design Rules

- Never crash on old registry format — always normalize gracefully
- `worktreePath` on old records becomes canonical; do NOT remove it
- Old records that happen to have `worktreePath` matching repo root should still normalize as `worktree` (they were created under worktree semantics)
- The normalization function should be explicit and testable, not buried in deserialization logic
- Add a `normalizeWorkerRecord(raw: unknown): WorkerRuntime` function

## Tests

- Old record without `executionMode` normalizes to `worktree`
- Old record with all legacy fields preserved after normalization
- New `current-branch` record passes through normalization unchanged
- New `worktree` record passes through normalization unchanged
- Registry load/save round-trip preserves normalized records
- Malformed/missing fields produce clear errors, not silent corruption

## Definition of Done

- `normalizeWorkerRecord` is exported and tested
- Registry load path uses normalization
- Existing worktree workers continue to work after normalization
- lint/test/typecheck pass clean
title: Normalize legacy registry records as worktree workers on load
